item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, and then I read your explanation and liked it even more. Wonderfully subtle; this is why SCPs don't always need to be long. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's wonderfully well written and interesting but I cannot, in good concience, upvote a scip that's vague to the point of being almost abstract, I'll actually downvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like some of the elements of this, but I don't believe we're given enough information to piece together the story for ourselves. And, having read the author's explanation, I'm kind of underwhelmed. This has potential, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I'm interpreting correctly, the bus is SCP-1221.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured that he and the woman are two different SCPs, forced to hunt each other for all eternity for some kind of crime, perhaps against death himself? Or maybe, until one kills/incinerates the other enough times. However, the title is "vendetta", implying some kind of personal animosity. But is it the hatred of the man, or of the entity causing all this to happen? Hmm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the old McDonald's campaign goes, I'm loving it. Only a few words to tell a much longer story. That's the mark of a master.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, in my opinion, is a great example of how less can be more. I guessed at what was going on (and was right, which made me feel stupidly proud), but it's so much better that it COULD be someone hunting the offspring of Lilith, or a crazy cloner playing The Most Dangerous Game, or any number of things. Another +1 for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Walker was actually a reference to the story "The Devil and Tom Walker". In this case, 1646 is stating that he resents the terms of his agreement with (literally) Satan, to the point that he would consider the fate of the man in that story an improvement.

But, if you want it to be that other SCP (whose number I don't know), go ahead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (found this by the story, so I'm adding some thought from there, too) Is Walker that other SCP that is following his ex-wife? It seems like this might be a competition between the two. One is chasing, one step behind; and the other is trying to kill, perhaps one person behind?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One who was simply angry might realize that upon careful consideration… thing is, serial killers have a strong tendency to be not right in the head. Killing could very well have been more or less a compulsion for him.

Or possibly he's just naturally a shortsighted, spiteful man. Or some combination of the two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A well done SCP, but honestly, forever? Even the most angry of angry dudes I think, should realize that eventually they're going to get tired of killing her over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. -1

EDIT: I mean, this is just too generic. A random killer, a random Freddy Kruger if you want. The same genetic code and the messages doesn't grab me at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It may be late, but if it makes you feel any better, I got the "transient" nature of the victim right off. I mean, it was ambiguous, but it was one of the possible explanations I had. The rest of the stuff about motivations and deals however is pretty much hinted at nowhere in the text. I thought he was a Native American monstrosity that had been born that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit that it's more than a bit obtuse, and that wasn't quite my intention when I wrote it. Looking back, though, I can't say I'm displeased with the end result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not what I had in mind when I wrote it, but what I think isn't really that important, is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is he hunting the children of Lilith, by any chance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm replying here so that I'll see the post.

I don't get it either, but that doesn't keep me from having enjoyed this. I can only guess it will be more amazing once it's explained.

Response to Spoilers
Edit!

No, no, that's cool as all get out. The letdown is that none of that comes through in the article itself.

Which still doesn't detract from the overall effect, mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say I love it too and I don't get it either. What does it for me is the lack of explanation and the general WTF-factor. That's hard to pull off without it being an annoyance. I'd love an spoiler of course, that won't diminish my enjoyment of this article, but I'd be very happy if this were never explained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was hoping to avoid that because it kinda takes some of the fun out of it for me… but I'll consider it. Might edit this post later tonight with a (spoilered) explanation.

EDIT: My explanation, feel free to come up with your own.

Ancient Aliens?
Nope.

A long, long time ago, a man was in love with a woman. The woman did something that pissed him off beyond belief. He made a deal with the devil or some similar entity ("Walker had the better deal by far." is a reference to this deal via a reference to Tom Walker and the Devil, which I was reading in English at the time) in order to get back at her for what she did.

The details of this deal were that Mr. Angrydude would get the opportunity to kill this woman in every different reality/universe/dimension/whatever. These corpses are known to the Foundation as SCP-1646-2; they all come from a different universe. In exchange for the deal, Mr. Angrydude has to dispose of the bodies in our universe at a place of his choosing, to prove that he's still doing his work. He chose the West Coast of the United States because, at that time, that must have seemed like the edge of the universe.

Also he gets to be immortal to get to carry this out, and as a side-effect of, y'know, interdimensional travel, we get the physical symptoms that he experiences, primarily the albinism and bleeding blue. Although the latter was actually a pun on blue blood originally, since my headcanon originally has this guy as a member of the upper class, so this might count as back-justification.

Probably not as cool as y'all were hoping for, but it's what I got.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wich scps are those?… well barely understand, but i liked (+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the slow of mind among us, could somebody spell it right out?

That said, I know good writing when I see it, so +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just so… subtle. All the evidence is there, but you don't outright state what it is. I love that kind of thing, where the reader has to put the pieces together himself. Like the bus, and the ancient containment site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I hate to sound self-indulgent, but why do you love it? I mainly want to know because I haven't gotten such a positive response on this one from anyone else, and I want to know what I'm doing right, so I can keep doing that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this thing. I really do.

item.title: Re: A thought
item.content:encoded: Hm… I like the idea. I'll give him a line of French, maybe.

item.title: A thought
item.content:encoded: It occurs to me that perhaps this guy should not be speaking English. Or not only English, maybe. Estonian, maybe, or Xhosa, and occasionally Khmer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never heard of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you mean Jet Li…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahhh, gotcha. That means he's going somewhere, not just vanishing and reappearing over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are now thinking of Bruce Li's "The One".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally assumed that he'd wear whatever was appropriate for wherever he was, so I just put in that sometimes he wore normal things and sometimes he didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe explain that he has one outfit with corresponding damage, or note any changes of clothing…

I asked because I just can't imagine either of them wearing anything significant for some reason. Personally in my head I see the guy wearing nothing but a ratty pair of jeans and being totally emaciated as he shuffles around. They strike me as both being a bit ghoulish in appearance, wearing scraps if anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to guess that 1646-1 wears clothes, although I'm not sure how to state that. "SCP-1646-1 is typically seen wearing clothing" sounds a bit weird.

Good call on the specimens thing. I'll do that.

And as for whether they're individuals, the answer is "sort of". It's one individual, lots of times over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do either of them wear clothes?

Also, for 1646-2, I suggest not using the term "individuals" — use "specimens" instead. Hell, the impression I get is that these are NOT individuals anyway, being almost always genetically identical and not corresponding to any missing people

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, it doesn't catch me in any visceral sort of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This idea popped into my head as I was walking home from school a few weeks ago. I hope I've provided sufficient clues to give you a general idea of what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I put my two cents in in chat. I lieks it.

I don't know why the idea of bleeding blue is creepy to me. But it got you an upvote.
